methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Biotech & Health (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Biotech & Health, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.9x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Biotech & Health

data.narrative:
ABL Bio is a public-stage biotech company (KOSDAQ-listed) founded by Sang Hoon Lee, having raised over $5B in combined IPO and licensing proceeds. The valuation analysis draws from a universe of 3 public comparables in Biotech & Health (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private peers including Colossal Biosciences, Abridge, Chai Discovery, New Limit, and Recursion Pharmaceuticals.

Two standard triangulation methods were evaluated but proved inapplicable given data constraints. Peer-median analysis was rejected due to insufficient narrow peer matches—while 8 same-sector private companies exist, none met simultaneous filters for stage, timing, and valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring could not proceed: the Biotech & Health sector median P/S stands at 6.9x across the three public tickers, but ABL Bio's revenue figures are not on file, precluding multiplication-based valuation.

Without disclosed revenue or a sufficiently tight peer set meeting stage and tier criteria, quantitative triangulation cannot yield a defensible range. The analysis framework identifies this as a data-availability problem rather than a methodological one. To support a valuation narrative, ABL Bio would need to either disclose revenue guidance or be paired against private comparables with documented recent funding rounds and similar clinical-stage positioning. Current information supports only a qualified pass pending these inputs.
